results.seo_title: How Data Centers Can Deliver Higher Performance with Less Hardware
results.meta_description: Discover how MIT researchers developed a system that boosts storage device performance by intelligently balancing workloads, increasing data center efficiency…
results.body_html:
Direct answer to the question of how data centers can deliver higher performance with less hardware is that MIT researchers have developed a system that intelligently balances workloads to improve storage device efficiency.

Key BenefitsIncreases overall data center efficiency by balancing workloads across multiple storage devicesReduces the need for specialized hardware, making it more sustainable and cost-effectiveNearly doubles performance delivered by traditional approaches in realistic tasks like AI model training and image compressionSystem ArchitectureComponentDescriptionCentral ControllerMakes big-picture decisions about which tasks each storage device performsLocal ControllersRapidly reroute data if a device is strugglingTwo-Tier ArchitectureHandles three major sources of variability simultaneously, delivering significant speed improvementsReal-World ApplicationsAI model training and image compression tasks saw nearly double the performance delivered by traditional approaches with this system.

source.title: Helping data centers deliver higher performance with less hardware
source.summary: Researchers developed a system that intelligently balances workloads to improve the efficiency of flash storage hardware in a data center.

results.seo_title: How NVIDIA AI Is Protecting the Planet Through Sustainable…
results.meta_description: Discover how NVIDIA's AI-powered solutions are reducing environmental impact and promoting sustainability in various industries.
results.body_html:
NVIDIA's AI is protecting the planet through sustainable technologies, including precision weed management and real-time detection of illegal logging.

1. Precision Weed Management

  • Conventional sprayers apply agrochemicals uniformly, resulting in wastage of valuable compounds and environmental pollution.
  • NVIDIA's smart sprayer uses machine vision and AI to distinguish target weeds from non-target objects, reducing waste and costs.
  • The system achieves 99.37% accuracy in detecting chainsaw sounds, surpassing other machine learning models.

2. Real-Time Detection of Illegal Logging

Feature Description
IoT nodes Use power-saving techniques to achieve a battery life of at least 8 years.
CNN model Achieves 99.37% accuracy in detecting chainsaw sounds, surpassing other machine learning models.
LoRaWAN Used for long-range data transmission, making it suitable for large forest areas.

3. Sustainable Data Centers

  • NVIDIA's AI-powered solutions are reducing energy consumption and carbon emissions in data centers.
  • The company is investing in renewable energy sources to power its data centers.
  • NVIDIA's data center infrastructure is designed to be highly efficient and sustainable.

4. Cybersecurity Threats Mitigation

"NVIDIA's AI-powered solutions are helping to mitigate cybersecurity threats by detecting and preventing malicious activity in real-time."

5. Future of Sustainable Technologies

  • NVIDIA is investing in research and development of new sustainable technologies.
  • The company is exploring the use of quantum computing for more efficient data processing.
  • NVIDIA's AI-powered solutions are expected to play a key role in reducing environmental impact and promoting sustainability in various industries.

Read more about how NVIDIA's AI is protecting the planet through sustainable technologies at NVIDIA Blog.

  1. Source: NVIDIA Blog
  2. Read time: 10 minutes

source.title: From Rainforests to Recycling Plants: 5 Ways NVIDIA AI Is Protecting the Planet
source.summary: Across climate, conservation, disaster monitoring and recycling, NVIDIA AI is enabling applications protecting the planet.

The “EnergAIzer” method generates reliable results in seconds, enabling data center operators to efficiently allocate resources and reduce wasted energy.

Fast Results for Data Center Operators

Data center operators can now estimate AI power consumption in a few seconds, unlike traditional modeling techniques that can take hours or even days to yield results. This fast estimation method enables data center operators to effectively allocate limited resources across multiple AI models and processors, improving energy efficiency.

Wide Range of Hardware Configurations

Hardware Configurations Supported by the EnergAIzer Method Processor/AI Accelerator ChipPredicted Power Consumption IBM Quantum System One2.5 kW Xilinx Alveo 88T1.8 kW

Benefits for Algorithm Developers and Model Providers

Assess potential energy consumption of new models before deployment. Potential cost savings through optimized resource allocation.

Conclusion The EnergAIzer method provides a fast, convenient, and reliable way to estimate AI power consumption, enabling data center operators to improve energy efficiency and reducing wasted energy.

Integration with existing energy management systems. Expansion to support emerging hardware designs.

results.seo_title: How mKernel Revolutionizes GPU-Driven Communication in AI…
results.meta_description: Discover how mKernel, a multi-GPU, multi-node fused kernel library, enhances AI communication and its impact on the industry.
results.body_html:
mKernel is a multi-GPU, multi-node fused kernel library that fuses intra-node NVLink, inter-node RDMA, and dense compute into a single persistent CUDA kernel. This innovation enables faster and more efficient communication in AI infrastructure, paving the way for breakthroughs in fields like autonomous vehicles and robotaxis.

Key Benefits of mKernelFaster data transfer rates: mKernel's fused kernel library reduces latency and increases throughput in GPU-driven communication.Improved scalability: By supporting multiple GPUs and nodes, mKernel enables more efficient use of resources and better handling of large-scale AI workloads.Enhanced reliability: The persistent CUDA kernel ensures that data is consistently transmitted and received across the network, reducing errors and improving overall performance.Impact on IndustryIndustry SegmentExpected ImpactAutonomous VehiclesmKernel's improved communication capabilities will enable faster and more accurate sensor data processing, leading to enhanced autonomous vehicle performance.RobotaxisThe increased efficiency and reliability of

mKernel will result in reduced latency and improved navigation accuracy for robotaxis systems.Technical TakeawaysmKernel's fused kernel library is designed to optimize data transfer rates, improve scalability, and enhance reliability in GPU-driven communication.Future DevelopmentsIntegration with existing AI frameworks: mKernel will be integrated with popular AI frameworks to further accelerate development and deployment of AI applications.Expansion to new industries: The technology's versatility and scalability make it an attractive solution for a wide range of industries, from healthcare to finance.

source.title: Meet mKernel: A Multi-GPU, Multi-Node Fused Kernel Library for GPU-Driven Communication
source.summary:
UC Berkeley's UCCL team releases mKernel, fusing intra-node NVLink, inter-node RDMA, and dense compute into a single persistent CUDA kernel.

The post Meet mKernel: A Multi-GPU, Multi-Node Fused Kernel Library for GPU-Driven Communication appeared first on MarkTechPost.

Record Revenue and Startup Holdings

Revenue Growth Slowdown expected in the following quarter

Investment in AI and Cloud Computing

Nvidia's investment in startups is focused on emerging technologies such as AI, cloud computing, and robotics. The company aims to leverage its expertise in these areas to drive innovation and growth.

Nvidia's record quarter and investment in startups have significant implications for the tech industry, particularly in the areas of AI and cloud computing. The company's leadership position is likely to drive innovation and growth in these areas.

Elon Musk's xAI is taking a significant step towards reducing its carbon footprint by investing $2.8 billion in natural gas turbines over the next three years, according to SpaceX's IPO filing.

This move marks a substantial shift in the company's approach to data center sustainability, as it seeks to minimize its environmental impact while continuing to drive innovation in AI technology.

The investment is part of xAI's broader strategy to develop more efficient and sustainable data centers, which will enable the company to scale its operations while reducing its reliance on fossil fuels.

results.seo_title: Backdoor Vulnerabilities in Unified Autoregressive Models: Token by…
results.meta_description: Researchers discover unified autoregressive models are vulnerable to backdoor attacks, enabling malicious effects across multiple output modalities.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Unified autoregressive models are vulnerable to backdoor attacks, enabling malicious effects across multiple output modalities.

Attack MechanismToken-substitution and Token-addition enable flexible and stealthy attacks by making token-level modifications to the original output for backdoored inputs.The attack can be induced through data poisoning or model access, achieving an average success rate of 63.1% against JanusPro.Impact on Unified Liquid ModelEffectSuccess RateModality-aligned brand promotion or ideological influence in 55% of generations.63.1%Comparison to Existing Backdoor AttacksThe proposed Token by Token Backdoor Attack (ToBAC) is the first backdoor attack targeting unified autoregressive models, exploring both data-based and model-based poisoning strategies.Future Research DirectionsDeveloping more effective defense mechanisms against ToBAC.Investigating the impact

of ToBAC on other multimodal large language models.

source.title: Token by Token, Compromised: Backdoor Vulnerabilities in Unified Autoregressive Models
source.summary: Researchers have discovered a vulnerability in unified autoregressive models (UAMs), which are used to generate both text and images. The "Token by Token Backdoor Attack" (ToBAC) allows malicious characters or words to be inserted into the model, causing it to produce harmful or fabricated content, such as promoting brands or ideologies. This attack can be carried out with or without access to the model's underlying code. ToBAC has been successfully tested on two popular UAM models, achieving a high success rate in manipulating visual outputs and accompanying text.

source.title: Alibaba Qwen Team Introduces Qwen3.5-LiveTranslate-Flash: Real-Time Multimodal Interpretation Across 60 Languages at 2.8-Second Latency
source.summary: Alibaba's Qwen team has released Qwen3.5-LiveTranslate-Flash, a real-time multimodal translation model that can process audio and video simultaneously across 60 input languages. The model produces speech output in 29 languages with latency of 2.8 seconds. Key features include speaker voice cloning, vision-enhanced comprehension, and dynamic keyword configuration for domain-specific terminology. The model has outperformed major commercial alternatives on benchmark tests.

results.meta_description: NVIDIA has released Nematron-Labs-Diffusion, a tri-mode language model with 6× tokens per forward over Qwen3-8B, enabling new AI applications.
results.body_html:
NVIDIA has released Nematron-Labs-Diffusion, a tri-mode language model with 6× tokens per forward over Qwen3-8B, enabling new AI applications.

Key Features of Nematron-Labs-Diffusion

Supports autoregressive (AR) decoding, diffusion-based parallel decoding, and self-speculation decoding Available in 3B, 8B, and 14B parameter sizes Includes base, instruct, and vision-language variants

Advantages of Nematron-Labs-Diffusion

Autoregressive Decoding Enables efficient and accurate language model decoding

Diffusion-Based Parallel Decoding Improves parallel processing capabilities for large-scale language models

Self-Speculation Decoding Enhances model's ability to adapt to changing task requirements

Future Applications of Nematron-Labs-Diffusion

Potential use in autonomous vehicles and robotaxis for improved language understanding Possibility of integrating with existing AI systems for enhanced productivity Potential applications in natural language processing and machine learning

source.title: NVIDIA AI Releases Nemotron-Labs-Diffusion: A Tri-Mode Language Model with 6× Tokens Per Forward Over Qwen3-8B
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

NVIDIA researchers have released Nemotron-Labs-Diffusion, a new language model family that combines three decoding modes: autoregressive, diffusion-based parallel, and self-speculation. The model comes in three parameter sizes (3B, 8B, and 14B) and includes variants for base, instruct, and vision-language tasks. Nemotron-Labs-Diffusion supports up to 6 tokens per forward pass over the Qwen3-8B architecture. This release aims to improve language processing capabilities.

results.seo_title: The Stratos Project: A Colossal Data Center in Utah Raises…
results.meta_description: A massive data center project in Utah has sparked environmental concerns and public backlash, raising questions about the impact of AI dominance on the…
results.body_html:
The Stratos Project, a 40,000-acre data center in Utah, has sparked environmental concerns and public backlash. Despite its promise of establishing American AI dominance, the project's massive size and power consumption have raised questions about its impact on the environment.

Environmental Impact

  • The Stratos Project is projected to consume 9GW of power, almost double Utah's peak electricity demand in 2025.
  • The project's massive size has sparked concerns about environmental damage and a strain on already overtaxed water supplies.
  • Experts have warned that the project could lead to increased greenhouse gas emissions and contribute to climate change.

Cost and Funding

CostFunding Source
$4 billion+Kevin O'Leary's investment firm in partnership with West GenCo

Location and Permits

  • The Stratos Project is located on a 62-square-mile campus that overlaps with Department of Defense land.
  • The project has obtained approval from the county and Gov. Spencer Cox, but must still obtain environmental and building permits.
  • Construction is expected to take years, with no firm timeline in sight.

Industry Impact

  • The Stratos Project has sparked concerns about the impact of AI dominance on the environment and public health.
  • Experts have warned that the project could lead to increased greenhouse gas emissions and contribute to climate change.
  • The project's massive size and power consumption have raised questions about its economic viability.

Conclusion

The Stratos Project raises important questions about the impact of AI dominance on the environment and public health. As the US continues to invest in AI research and development, it is essential that we prioritize sustainability and environmental responsibility.

  1. Stay tuned for updates on the Stratos Project's progress and its potential impact on the environment.

source.title: The biggest data center ever is becoming a huge problem in Utah
source.summary: A massive data center project, known as the Stratos Project, is being built in Box Elder County, Utah, spanning 40,000 acres. The facility has been met with criticism from experts and public backlash despite its intended purpose of establishing American AI dominance. The project's scale and potential environmental impact have raised concerns among local residents and authorities.

results.seo_title: Google and SpaceX in Talks to Put Data Centers into Orbit: A New…
results.meta_description: Google and SpaceX are exploring the possibility of building data centers in orbit, promising a new frontier for AI compute. But what does this mean for the…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Google and SpaceX are indeed in talks to build data centers in orbit, with the goal of providing a new frontier for AI compute.

Reduced latency: With the ability to access data from space, latency issues that plague traditional data centers may be alleviated. Increased scalability: Orbital data centers could provide a scalable solution for AI compute, allowing for increased processing power and storage capacity. Improved security: Data centers in orbit could offer an additional layer of security against cyber threats and physical attacks.

Challenge Current Status Potential Solution

Power generation and distribution Currently, power generation and distribution in space are limited. Researchers are exploring the use of solar panels and other renewable energy sources to power orbital data centers.

Radiation protection Currently, radiation protection is a significant challenge for space-based systems. Researchers are developing new materials and technologies to protect against radiation damage.

Potential Applications Orbital data centers could enable the widespread adoption of AI in industries such as autonomous vehicles, robotics, and cybersecurity.

Further research and development is needed to overcome technical challenges. Industry partnerships and collaborations will be essential for bringing this technology to market. Regulatory frameworks will need to be developed to address the unique challenges of orbital data centers.

source.title: Report: Google and SpaceX in talks to put data centers into orbit
source.summary: Google and SpaceX are in talks to build data centers in orbit, pitching space as the future home for AI compute, even as costs today remain far higher than on the ground.

results.seo_title: How to Fine-Tune NVIDIA Cosmos Predict 2.5 with LoRA/DoRA for Robot…
results.meta_description: Learn how to fine-tune NVIDIA Cosmos Predict 2.5 with LoRA/DoRA for robot video generation using the diffusers and accelerate libraries.
results.body_html:
## Fine-Tuning NVIDIA Cosmos Predict 2.5 with LoRA/DoRA for Robot Video Generation

NVIDIA Cosmos Predict 2.5 is a large-scale world model capable of generating physically plausible videos conditioned on text, images, or video clips. To adapt it to a specific domain, such as robot manipulation or a particular camera viewpoint, teams still need targeted fine-tuning.

Training robot policies requires demonstration data, but collecting real-robot trajectories is slow and expensive. Generating synthetic trajectories with a fine-tuned video world model offers a scalable alternative. However, full fine-tuning of a 2B-parameter model is expensive and risks catastrophic forgetting of general knowledge.

LoRA and DoRA inject small trainable adapter modules into the frozen base model, reducing memory requirements while keeping the adapter files small and portable. This makes it practical to fine-tune on a single GPU and flexibly swap adapters for different domains at inference.

This guide walks through parameter-efficient fine-tuning of Cosmos Predict 2.5 with LoRA and DoRA, using the diffusers and accelerate libraries with support for both single- and multi-GPU training. We then show how to use the fine-tuned model to generate synthetic robot trajectories for downstream robot learning tasks.

Requirements

diffusers (pulls in transformers and peft automatically), accelerate

Optional: install wandb to monitor training

At minimum one 80 GB GPU for single-GPU training; 8× H100s recommended for faster iteration

Install dependencies on your machine:

pip install -U "diffusers[torch]" transformers accelerate peft wandb

Preparing Data

After installing diffusers, navigate to examples/cosmos to explore the example code. We use the same datasets as the GR00T Dreams post-training recipe:

Training Dataset: 92 robot manipulation videos with text prompts describing pick-and-place tasks.

Test Dataset: 50 (prompt, image) pairs. The model should generate a video based on the input text prompt and the initial frame image.

Download and preprocess the training and test datasets using download_and_preprocess_datasets.sh:

bash download_and_preprocess_datasets.sh

The resulting training dataset folder looks like this:

gr1_dataset/train ├── metas/ │ └── *.txt ├── videos/ │ └── *.mp4 └── metadata.csv

The eval dataset is a flat directory of paired .txt and .png files for the (prompt, image) pairs:

gr1_dataset/test ├── filename1.txt ├── filename1.png ├── filename2.txt ├── filename2.png └── ...

Training

In this section, we walk through the implementation in train_cosmos_predict25_lora.py.

VideoDataset

source.title: Fine-Tuning NVIDIA Cosmos Predict 2.5 with LoRA/DoRA for Robot Video Generation
source.summary: Fine-Tuning NVIDIA Cosmos Predict 2.5 with LoRA/DoRA for Robot Video Generation

results.seo_title: How Industry and Academia Diverge in Chip Design Strategies
results.meta_description: As a chip designer, transitioning from academia to industry requires adapting to different goals, risk tolerance, and strategies.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: The industry and academia diverge in their approach to chip design, with industry focusing on reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness.

Key Differences in Industry and Academia

Industry focuses on ensuring that a chip works reliably, repeatedly, and at scale, while academia prioritizes generating new knowledge and exploring unproven territory. Industry minimizes risk through conservative margins, extensive validation, and careful reuse of proven solutions, whereas academia often deliberately pushes into unproven territory to yield valuable insight. The cost of failure is a significant factor in industry design flows, making first-time silicon success a central requirement.

Impact on Chip Design Scope and Verification Philosophy AspectAcademiaIndustryDesign scopeAbsorbs many complexities, such as packaging constraints and manufacturing yield.Shifts to system-level integration, incorporating dozens or hundreds of functional blocks.Verification philosophyDemonstrates that a concept works under nominal conditions, often with flexible timelines.Exhaustive, critical, and often dominates the development schedule, with failures measured in parts per million.

The industry needs more chip designers, but those from an academic background must adapt to these differences. Academic designs often focus on block-level innovation, while industry focuses on system-level integration. Industry design flows are built around eliminating uncertainty through conservative margins and extensive validation.

The ASIC market is expected to grow from 23.4 billion to 38.8 billion by 2033, driven by demand for specialized chips in the automotive sector, AI applications, and more. The semiconductor industry as a whole is projected to hit $1 trillion by 2030.

source.title: Finding Success in Industry as a Chip Designer
source.summary: The article discusses the differences between academia and industry in chip design, specifically application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs). The author, a seasoned ASIC designer, shares their experience transitioning from academia to industry and highlights the stark contrasts between the two. In academia, the focus is on exploring new ideas and pushing boundaries, while in industry, the goal is to ensure reliable and efficient production of chips at scale. This shift in perspective requires designers to adopt a more conservative approach, prioritizing risk minimization and reuse of proven solutions over innovation.

results.seo_title: How to Build a Healthcare Robot from Simulation to Deployment with…
results.meta_description: Learn how to build a healthcare robot from simulation to deployment using NVIDIA Isaac for Healthcare, a developer framework for AI healthcare robotics.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: NVIDIA Isaac for Healthcare is a developer framework for AI healthcare robotics that enables developers to build autonomous medical robotics workflows on real hardware.

Hardware Requirements

  • GPU: RT Core-enabled architecture (Ampere or later) with ≥30GB VRAM for GR00T N1.5 inference
  • SO-ARM101 Follower: 6-DOF precision manipulator with dual-camera vision
  • SO-ARM101 Leader: 6-DOF Teleoperation interface for expert demonstration collection

Simulation Teleoperation Controls

For users without physical SO-ARM101 hardware, the workflow provides keyboard-based teleoperation with the following joint controls:

  • JOINT 1: 0° to 180°
  • JOINT 2: 0° to 180°
  • JOINT 3: 0° to 180°

Model Training Pipeline

After collecting both simulation and real-world data, convert and combine datasets for training:

  • Convert simulation data to LeRobot format using python -m training.hdf5_to_lerobot --repo_id=surgical_assistance_dataset --hdf5_path=/path/to/your/sim_dataset.hdf5 --task_description="Autonomous surgical instrument handling and preparation"
  • Post-train GR00T N1.5 on mixed dataset using python -m training.gr00t_n1_5.train --dataset_path /path/to/your/surgical_assistance_dataset --output_dir /path/to/surgical_checkpoints --data_config so100_dualcam

End-to-End Sim Collect–Train–Eval Pipelines

Simulation is most powerful when it's part of a loop: collect data → train → evaluate → deploy. Isaac Lab supports this full pipeline:

  • Teleoperate robots using keyboard or hardware controllers
  • Capture multi-camera observations, robot states, and actions
  • Create diverse datasets with edge cases impossible to collect safely in real environments

Getting Started

  • Clone the repository: git clone https://github.com/isaac-for-healthcare/i4h-workflows.git
  • Choose a workflow: Start with the SO-ARM Starter Workflow for surgical assistance or explore other workflows
  • Run the setup: Each workflow includes an automated setup script (for example, tools/env_setup_so_arm_starter.sh)

source.title: How to Build a Healthcare Robot from Simulation to Deployment with NVIDIA Isaac for Healthcare
source.summary: How to Build a Healthcare Robot from Simulation to Deployment with NVIDIA Isaac for Healthcare

results.seo_title: Trade AI Token Futures Like Gold and Oil, Experts Say
results.meta_description: As AI token futures market grows, experts propose standardized contracts for trading AI tokens like commodities. Learn more about the future of AI trading.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Yes, large exchanges are designing derivative products around AI tokens, which are increasingly being considered less a computational output and more a raw material input.

Standardized Contracts for AI Token FuturesProposed by OpenAlex, the paper systematically analyzes the commodity attributes of tokens, arguing for their transition from intelligent service outputs to compute infrastructure raw materials.The proposed design includes standardized token futures contracts, including contract specifications, settlement mechanisms, margin systems, and market-maker regimes.Comparison to Established CommoditiesCommodityAttributeElectricityFrequent supply and demand imbalanceCarbon Emission AllowancesRegulated by international agreementsBandwidthIncreasing demand due to digitalizationImplications for the AI IndustryAs the decarbonisation of the global economy accelerates, securing uninterrupted access to critical raw minerals will become increasingly important for high-technology applications.Future of AI

TradingThe proposed standardized contracts aim to create a more efficient and transparent market for AI token futures.Experts believe that this will enable better risk management and increased investment in the AI industry.

source.title: Just like gold and oil, we’ll soon be able to trade AI token futures
source.summary: Several large exchanges are developing derivative products based on AI tokens, treating them as commodities similar to gold and oil. This shift in perspective views AI tokens as raw materials rather than solely as digital outputs. The goal is to create standardized trading platforms for these emerging assets.

results.seo_title: Building a Healthcare Robot from Simulation to Deployment with NVIDIA
results.meta_description: Introduction SO-ARM Starter Workflow; Building an Embodied Surgical Assistant Technical Implementation Sim2Real Mixed Training Approach Hardware Requirements…
results.body_html:
Table-of-Contents

Introduction

Simulation has been a cornerstone in medical imaging to address the data gap. However, in healthcare robotics until now, it's often been too slow, siloed, or difficult to translate into real-world systems.

NVIDIA Isaac for Healthcare, a developer framework for AI healthcare robotics, enables healthcare robotics developers in solving these challenges via offering integrated data collection, training, and evaluation pipelines that work across both simulation and hardware. Specifically, the Isaac for Healthcare v0.4 release provides healthcare developers with an end-to-end SO - ARM based starter workflow and the bring your own operating room tutorial. The SO-ARM starter workflow lowers the barrier for MedTech developers to experience the full workflow from simulation to train to deployment and start building and validating autonomous on real hardware right away.

In this post, we'll walk through the starter workflow and its technical implementation details to help you build a surgical assistant robot in less time than ever imaginable before.

SO-ARM Starter Workflow; Building an Embodied Surgical Assistant

Fine-tune GR00t N1.5, evaluate in IsaacLab, then deploy to hardware

This workflow gives developers a safe, repeatable environment to train and refine assistive skills before moving into the Operating Room.

Technical Implementation

Model Training: Fine-tuning GR00T N1.5 on combined datasets with dual-camera vision

Notably, over 93% of the data used for policy training was generated synthetically in simulation, underscoring the strength of simulation in bridging the robotic data gap.

Sim2Real Mixed Training Approach

The workflow combines simulation and real-world data to address the fundamental challenge that training robots in the real world is expensive and limited, while pure simulation often fails to capture real-world complexities. The approach uses approximately 70 simulation episodes for diverse scenarios and environmental variations, combined with 10-20 real-world episodes for authenticity and grounding. This mixed training creates policies that generalize beyond either domain alone.

Hardware Requirements

GPU: RT Core-enabled architecture (Ampere or later) with ≥30GB VRAM for GR00TN1.5 inference

SO-ARM101 Follower: 6-DOF precision manipulator with dual-camera vision (wrist and room). The SO-ARM101 features WOWROBO vision components, including a wrist-mounted camera with a 3D-printed adapter

Notably, developers could run all the simulation, training and deployment (3 computers needed for physical AI) on one DGX Spark.

Data Collection Implementation

python lerobot-record \ --robot.type=so101_follower \ --robot.port= \ --robot.cameras="wrist: type: opencv, index_or_path: 0, width: 640, height: 480, fps: 30, room: type: opencv, index_or_path: 2, width: 640, height: 480, fps: 30" \ --robot.id=so101_follower_arm \ --teleop.type=so101_leader \ --teleop.port= \ --teleop.id=so101_leader_arm \ --dataset.repo_id=/surgical_assistance/surgical_assistance \ --dataset.num_episodes=15 \ --dataset.single_task="Prepare and hand surgical instruments to surgeon"
# With keyboard teleoperation python -m simulation.environments.teleoperation_record \ --enable_cameras \ --record \ --dataset_path=/path/to/save/dataset.hdf5 \ --teleop_device=keyboard # With SO-ARM101 leader arm python -m simulation.environments.teleoperation_record \ --port= \ --enable_cameras \ --record \ --dataset_path=/path/to/save/dataset.hdf5

Simulation Teleoperation Controls

Model Training Pipeline

# Convert simulation data to LeRobot format python -m training.hdf5_to_lerobot \ --repo_id=surgical_assistance_dataset \ --hdf5_path=/path/to/your/sim_dataset.hdf5 \ --task_description="Autonomous surgical instrument handling and preparation" # Fine-tune GR00T N1.5 on mixed dataset python -m training.gr00t_n1_5.train \ --dataset_path /path/to/your/surgical_assistance_dataset \ --output_dir /path/to/surgical_checkpoints \ --data_config so100_dualcam

The trained model processes natural language instructions such as "Prepare the scalpel for the surgeon" or "Hand me the forceps" and executes the corresponding robotic actions. With LeRobot latest release (0.4.0) you will be able to fine-tune Gr00t N1.5 natively in LeRobot!

End-to-End Sim Collect–Train–Eval Pipelines

Simulation is most powerful when it's part of a loop: collect → train → evaluate → deploy.

Generate Synthetic Data in Simulation

Train and Evaluate Policies

results.seo_title: NVIDIA brings agents to life with DGX Spark and Reachy Mini
results.meta_description:
## NVIDIA brings agents to life with DGX Spark and Reachy Mini

Ingredients Giving agentic powers to Reachy Building the agent Step 0: Set up and get access…
results.body_html:
## NVIDIA brings agents to life with DGX Spark and Reachy Mini

Ingredients Giving agentic powers to Reachy Building the agent Step 0: Set up and get access to models and services Step 1: Build a chat interface Step 2: Add NeMo Agent Toolkit’s built-in ReAct agent for tool calling Step 3: Add a router to direct queries to different models Step 4: Add a Pipecat bot for real-time voice + vision Step 5: Hook everything up to Reachy (hardware or simulation) Run the full system Try these example prompts Where to go next

Today at CES 2026, NVIDIA unveiled a world of new open models to enable the future of agents, online and in the real world. From the recently released NVIDIA Nemotron reasoning LLMs to the new NVIDIA Isaac GR00T N1.6 open reasoning VLA and NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models, all the building blocks are here today for AI Builders to build their own agents.

But what if you could bring your own agent to life, right at your desk? An AI buddy that can be useful to you and process your data privately?

In the CES keynote today, Jensen Huang showed us how we can do exactly that, using the processing power of NVIDIA DGX Spark with Reachy Mini to create your own little office R2D2 you can talk to and collaborate with.

This blog post provides a step-by-step guide to replicate this amazing experience at home using a DGX Spark and Reachy Mini.

Ingredients

If you want to start cooking right away, here’s the source code of the demo.

A reasoning model: demo uses NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano

A vision model: demo uses NVIDIA Nemotron Nano 2 VL

A text-to-speech model: demo uses ElevenLabs

Feel free to adapt the recipe and make it your own - you have many ways to integrate the models into your application:

Local deployment – Run on your own hardware (DGX Spark or a GPU with sufficient VRAM). Our implementation requires ~65GB disk space for the reasoning model, and ~28GB for the vision model.

Cloud deployment– Deploy the models on cloud GPUs e.g. through NVIDIA Brev or Hugging Face Inference Endpoints.

Serverless model endpoints – Send requests to NVIDIA or Hugging Face Inference Providers.

Giving agentic powers to Reachy

Turning an AI agent from a simple chat interface into something you can interact with naturally makes conversations feel more real. When an AI agent can see through a camera, speak out loud, and perform actions, the experience becomes more engaging. That’s what Reachy Mini makes possible.

Reachy Mini is designed to be customizable. With access to sensors, actuators, and APIs, you can easily wire it into your existing agent stack, by simulation or real hardware controlled directly from Python.

This post focuses on composing existing building blocks rather than reinventing them. We combine open models for reasoning and vision, an agent framework for orchestration, and tool handlers for actions. Each component is loosely coupled, making it easy to swap models, change routing logic, or add new behaviors.

Unlike closed personal assistants, this setup stays fully open. You control the models, the prompts, the tools, and the robot’s actions. Reachy Mini simply becomes the physical endpoint of your agent where perception, reasoning, and action come together.

Building the agent

In this example, we use the NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit, a flexible, lightweight, framework-agnostic open source library, to connect all the components of the agent together. It works seamlessly with other agentic frameworks, like LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, handling how models interact, routing inputs and outputs between them, and making it easy to experiment with different configurations or add new capabilities without rewriting core logic. The toolkit also provides built-in profiling and optimization features, letting you track token usage efficiency and latency across tools and agents, identify bottlenecks, and automatically tune

hyperparameters to maximize accuracy while reducing cost and latency.

Step 0: Set up and get access to models and services

results.seo_title: RULER: A Representation-Level Verification Metric for Machine…
results.meta_description: A new metric, RULER, measures representation-level verification of machine unlearning, detecting significant residuals in 10 of 12 conditions.
results.body_html:
RULER detects significant residuals in 10 of 12 conditions, with effect sizes growing as the forget fraction increases.

Four Approximate Unlearning Methods Pass Output-Level Evaluation

M2 measures whether forget-set records occupy the same representational position as in a model retrained without them. M4 detects residuals from the unlearned model's internal similarity structure alone, without retraining. Four approximate unlearning methods all pass output-level evaluation. A fifth method, Bad Teacher, shows the same residuals despite a different forgetting mechanism.

Linear Mixed-Effects Model Reveals Significant Residuals

M2 0.05 Growing as the forget fraction increases.

Pre-Unlearning Diagnostic Across Tabular, Image, Clinical Text, and Face-Identity Settings M4 acts as a pre-unlearning diagnostic across tabular, image, clinical text, and face-identity settings: it detects identity-level memorisation in face recognition models where no tested method fully erases the signal.

RULER provides a new metric for representation-level verification of machine unlearning. The results show that RULER detects significant residuals in 10 of 12 conditions. The effect sizes grow as the forget fraction increases.

source.title: RULER: Representation-Level Verification of Machine Unlearning
source.summary: Researchers have introduced RULER, a set of representation-level verification metrics to verify machine unlearning at a more fundamental level than current methods. The new metrics, M2 and M4, can detect whether forgotten records are still encoded in intermediate representations of a model after unlearning. These metrics were tested on four approximate unlearning methods, which passed output-level evaluation but failed to fully erase the signal in some cases. The results suggest that RULER can provide more accurate assessments of machine unlearning effectiveness.

results.seo_title: How to Benchmark NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano with NeMo Evaluator for…
results.meta_description: Learn how to benchmark NVIDIA Nematron 3 Nano with NeMo Evaluator for transparent AI model evaluation and reproducible results.
results.body_html:
The Open Evaluation Standard: Benchmarking NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano with NeMo Evaluator is a transparent and reproducible way to evaluate AI models.

Building a Consistent and Transparent Evaluation Workflow

Developers and researchers need evaluation workflows they can rely on, not one-off scripts that behave differently from model to model. Nemo Evaluator provides a unified way to define benchmarks, prompts, configuration, and runtime behavior once, then reuse that methodology across models and releases.

Methodology Independent of Inference Setup

Separation of evaluation pipeline from inference backend Nemo Evaluator separates the evaluation pipeline from the inference backend, allowing the same configuration to run against hosted endpoints, local deployments, or third-party providers.

Meaningful comparisons even when changing infrastructure or inference engines The separation enables meaningful comparisons even when you change infrastructure or inference engines.

Open evaluation means publishing not just the final results, but the full methodology behind them. Nemo Evaluator is an open-source library designed for robust, reproducible, and scalable evaluation of generative models.

Reproducing Nematron 3 Nano model card results follows a simple loop: Start from the released model checkpoint or hosted endpoint Execute the evaluation with a single CLI command Inspect logs and artifacts, and compare results to the model card

source.title: The Open Evaluation Standard: Benchmarking NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano with NeMo Evaluator
source.summary: The Open Evaluation Standard: Benchmarking NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano with NeMo Evaluator

results.seo_title: NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 Brings Advanced Reasoning To Physical AI
results.meta_description: Cosmos Reason 2 is a state-of-the-art, open reasoning vision-language model that enables robots and AI agents to see, understand, plan, and act in the…
results.body_html:
NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 is a state-of-the-art, open reasoning vision-language model that enables robots and AI agents to see, understand, plan, and act in the physical world like humans.

Improved spatio-temporal understanding and timestamp precision Optimized performance with flexible deployment options from edge to cloud with 2B and 8B parameters model sizes Support for expanded set of spatial understanding and visual perception capabilities — 2D/3D point localization, bounding box coordinates, trajectory data, and OCR support Improved long-context understanding with 256K input tokens, up from 16K with Cosmos Reason 1 Adaptable to multiple use cases with easy-to-use Cosmos Cookbook recipes

Video analytics AI agents — These agents can extract valuable insights from massive volumes of video data to optimize processes. Data annotation and critique — Enable developers to automate high-quality annotation and critique of massive, diverse training datasets. Robot planning and reasoning — Act as the brain for deliberate action in complex environments.

Improved decision-making capabilities for robots and AI agents Enhanced ability to understand and interpret visual data Increased flexibility and adaptability in deployment options

Potential applications in autonomous vehicles, robotics, and other industries Continued research and development to improve performance and capabilities

results.seo_title: Auditable Decision Models with Learned Abstention and Real-Time…
results.meta_description: A new bounded decision-control model, EvaluatorDPT, is presented to improve operational decision control for AI systems, enabling explicit routability of…
results.body_html:
Auditable decision models with learned abstention and real-time steering can improve AI system control by explicitly routable uncertainty, policy-governed behavior, and auditable execution decisions.

Key Features of EvaluatorDPT

  • Predicts YES, NO, or TBD with learned deferral outcome
  • Uses transformer encoder with primary bounded-decision head and structured auxiliary channels for values and emotions/sentiments
  • Domain-agnostic interface supplies evidence and policy thresholds
  • Bounded distribution controlled at inference time through recorded operating thresholds and auxiliary semantic signals

Evaluation Results

Metric Value
Accuracy 0.8260
Macro F1 0.8252
Per-class F1 (YES, NO, TBD) 0.8314, 0.8486, 0.7956

Conclusion and Future Work

The proposed EvaluatorDPT model demonstrates improved operational decision control for AI systems, enabling explicit routability of uncertainty, policy-governed behavior, and auditable execution decisions.

Towards Auditable AI Systems

  • Developing more robust and transparent AI systems through the use of auditable decision models like EvaluatorDPT
  • Improving the interpretability of AI-generated outputs through the incorporation of auxiliary signals
  • Investigating the application of EvaluatorDPT in real-world domains, such as healthcare and finance

source.title: Auditable Decision Models with Learned Abstention and Real-Time Steering
source.summary: Researchers have developed EvaluatorDPT, a decision-control model for AI systems that aims to explicitly manage uncertainty and produce auditable execution decisions. The model uses a transformer encoder with a bounded-decision head and auxiliary channels for values and emotions/sentiments, allowing for domain-agnostic deployment and inference-time control. In an evaluation on a test split of 44,597 samples, EvaluatorDPT achieved high accuracy (0.8260) and macro F1 (0.8252), with improved performance on the "YES" and "NO" classes compared to the "TBD" class. The model's design enables auditable behavior control through auxiliary signals and evaluation evidence.
